result.output:
Axial contrast-enhanced CT image of the upper abdomen.

Anatomy shown includes the liver, spleen, stomach/left upper quadrant, abdominal aorta, and vertebral body. There is a large rounded low-attenuation lesion or fluid-filled structure in the left upper abdomen adjacent to the stomach/spleen region. No free air or obvious acute osseous abnormality is visible on this single image.

modalities.text:
Axial contrast-enhanced CT image of the upper abdomen.

Anatomy shown includes the liver, spleen, stomach/left upper quadrant, abdominal aorta, and vertebral body. There is a large rounded low-attenuation lesion or fluid-filled structure in the left upper abdomen adjacent to the stomach/spleen region. No free air or obvious acute osseous abnormality is visible on this single image.

args.region: large rounded low-attenuation mass/fluid-filled lesion in the left upper abdomen adjacent to the stomach and spleen
large rounded low-attenuation mass/fluid-filled lesion in the left upper abdomen adjacent to the stomach and spleen

output.text: In the left upper abdomen adjacent to the stomach and spleen, there is a large rounded to ovoid low-attenuation lesion with fluid-like density. It has smooth, well-defined margins and appears homogeneous without visible internal solid components on this image. Its size is approximately 7–8 cm in greatest visible diameter, though exact measurement is limited from the single image.
